name: White Chocolate Berry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree and strain the raspberries.
2. Mix the crushed wafers and melted butter or margarine. Press into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Set aside.
3. In a small saucepan, combine the pureed raspberries, sugar, and cornstarch. Stir well over medium heat until thickened and bubbly. Cook for an additional 2 minutes. Set aside to cool.
4. Melt the white chocolate over low heat and set aside to cool.
5. In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese and 1/2 cup sugar with an electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until combined after each addition. Gradually beat in the melted white chocolate until blended.
6. Spread over the crumb crust. Spoon half of the raspberry mixture over the filling and swirl with a knife to marbleize. Cover and let the remaining raspberry sauce return to room temperature before serving.
7. Bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the center appears set when gently shaken. Do not overbake. Cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes.
8. Loosen the crust from the sides of the pan and cool for an additional 30 minutes. Remove the sides of the pan and let cool completely before chilling for at least 4 hours before slicing. Makes 12-16 servings.
